sections.summary: This Standard applies to sugars intended for direct human consumption or as ingredients and defines each product: e.g., white sugar (purified crystallised sucrose, polarisation ≥99.7 ºZ), plantation/mill white sugar (sucrose, polarisation ≥99.5 ºZ), powdered sugar (finely pulverised white sugar, ± anticaking agent), soft white sugar (moist, sucrose+invert ≥97.0% m/m), soft brown sugar (moist, sucrose+invert ≥88.0% m/m), dextrose anhydrous (D‑glucose ≥99.5% m/m dry, total solids ≥98.0% m/m), dextrose monohydrate (D‑glucose ≥99.5% m/m dry, total solids ≥90.0% m/m), powdered dextrose (pulverised dextrose ± anticaking agent), glucose syrup (nutritive saccharides from starch/inulin, dextrose equivalent ≥20.0% m/m, total solids ≥70.0% m/m), dried glucose syrup (total solids ≥93.0% m/m), lactose (anhydrous lactose ≥99.0% m/m dry), fructose (D‑fructose ≥98.0% m/m, glucose ≤0.5% m/m), and raw cane sugar (partially purified sucrose crystals coated with cane molasses).

Antioxidants and anticaking agents listed in Tables 1 and 2 of the General Standard for Food Additives (CXS 192-1995) are permitted in specified sugar categories (11.1.1, 11.1.2, 11.1.3, 11.1.5) when used in accordance with that Standard. Powdered sugar and powdered dextrose may contain up to 5% starch provided no anticaking agent is used.

Raw cane sugar must be free from heavy metals in amounts that may pose a health hazard, other sugars must comply with Codex maximum limits for heavy metals, and all products covered by this Standard must comply with Codex maximum residue limits for pesticide residues.

For raw cane sugar: must be free from heavy metals in amounts that may represent a hazard to human health; for other sugars: must comply with maximum heavy metal limits established by the Codex Alimentarius Commission.
Products covered by this Standard must comply with the maximum pesticide residue limits established by the Codex Alimentarius Commission for these commodities.

Products should be prepared and handled per the Codex General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969) and relevant Codes of Practice, and must meet any microbiological criteria established under the Codex Principles and Guidelines for Microbiological Criteria (CXG 21-1997).

In addition to the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre-packaged Foods (CXS 1-1985), this Standard requires that product names conform to Section 1 descriptions and includes specific name and descriptor rules (e.g., powdered dextrose must indicate anhydrous/monohydrate form; glucose syrup with >5% fructose must be described); the presence and maximum amount of starch must be declared on powdered sugar/powdered dextrose labels, and labelling of non-retail containers should follow CXS 346-2021.

All products must conform to the product descriptions in Section 1; powdered dextrose must bear a reference to dextrose anhydrous and/or monohydrate as appropriate, and glucose syrup containing more than 5% fructose must be described to reflect that.
The presence of starch and its maximum amount must be declared on the label or container of powdered sugar or powdered dextrose.
Labelling of non-retail containers should be carried out in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).

Compliance with this Standard shall be checked using the methods of analysis and sampling set out in the Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234-1999) relevant to the Standard's provisions.

sections.section: I. ADDITIONAL COMPOSITION AND QUALITY FACTORS
sections.summary: Composition and quality factors for the sugars covered by this Standard are specified in Table 1 of the Standard.
See the relevant Codex texts for additional methods of analysis and sampling applicable to this Standard.
